name: MS-QRD-002: Internal Network Port Scanning — Reconnaissance
description: Detects internal hosts performing horizontal port scanning against multiple destination IPs or vertical scanning against many ports on a single target. Identifies reconnaissance activity by counting distinct destination IP and port combinations per source within a 5-minute window. High unique destination counts from a single internal source indicate automated scanning tools such as Nmap, Masscan, or Cobalt Strike portscan modules.
author: MilliSec Blue Team — Rihad Ebilov

qradar.aql: SELECT sourceip AS "Source IP", LOGSOURCENAME(logsourceid) AS "Log Source", CATEGORYNAME(category) AS "Event Category", COUNT(DISTINCT destinationip) AS "Unique Target IPs", COUNT(DISTINCT destinationport) AS "Unique Target Ports", COUNT(*) AS "Total Connections", MIN(DATEFORMAT(starttime, 'yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss')) AS "First Seen", MAX(DATEFORMAT(starttime, 'yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss')) AS "Last Seen" FROM events WHERE (CATEGORYNAME(category) ILIKE '%port scan%' OR CATEGORYNAME(category) ILIKE '%network scan%' OR CATEGORYNAME(category) ILIKE '%connection refused%' OR CATEGORYNAME(category) ILIKE '%access attempt%') AND sourceip IS NOT NULL AND sourceip != '0.0.0.0' AND INCIDR('10.0.0.0/8', sourceip) OR INCIDR('172.16.0.0/12', sourceip) OR INCIDR('192.168.0.0/16', sourceip) GROUP BY sourceip, LOGSOURCENAME(logsourceid), CATEGORYNAME(category) HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT destinationip) >= 15 OR COUNT(DISTINCT destinationport) >= 20 ORDER BY "Unique Target IPs" DESC LAST 5 MINUTES
qradar.offense_relevance: Internal hosts initiating broad port scans are a strong indicator of lateral movement preparation, compromised insider, or malware propagation. Correlate with subsequent authentication events from the scanning source to detect active exploitation attempts following reconnaissance.
response_actions: Isolate scanning host from network if scan volume is extreme
Investigate running processes on source host via XDR
Check for newly spawned network utility processes (nmap, masscan, netcat)
Cross-reference source IP against known asset inventory
Add source IP to QRadar Reference Set: Internal_Scanners for 24h monitoring
qradar.false_positive_notes: Authorized vulnerability scanners (Nessus, Acunetix) running scheduled scans. Network monitoring tools (PRTG, Zabbix). IT asset discovery tools. Mitigate by adding known scanner IPs to a QRadar Reference Set whitelist.
qradar.tuning_notes: Adjust HAVING thresholds: COUNT(DISTINCT destinationip) >= 15 for horizontal scan detection, COUNT(DISTINCT destinationport) >= 20 for vertical. Whitelist authorized scanner IPs by adding AND sourceip NOT IN (SELECT value FROM REFERENCETABLE('Authorized_Scanners')) to WHERE clause after Reference Set is created.
